members.name: AddAzureFunctionsProject
members.signature: public static IResourceBuilder<AzureFunctionsProjectResource> AzureFunctionsProjectResourceExtensions.AddAzureFunctionsProject<TProject>(this IDistributedApplicationBuilder builder, string name)

summary.text: Adds an Azure Functions project to the distributed application.

children.text: This overload is not available in polyglot app hosts. Use
When Functions projects are deployed to Azure Container Apps, they are provisioned with the container app
. This enables KEDA auto-scaler rules to be automatically configured based on the Azure Functions triggers defined in the project.

By default, an implicit Azure Storage account is provisioned to be used as host storage for the Functions runtime. This storage account is required by the Azure Functions runtime for operations such as managing triggers, logging function executions, and coordinating instances. The implicit storage account is assigned the following roles:
to specify a custom Azure Storage resource as the host storage instead of the implicit default storage account.

returns.text: for the added Azure Functions project resource.

builder.text: to which the Azure Functions project will be added.

name.text: The name to be associated with the Azure Functions project. This name will be used for service discovery when referenced in a dependency.

TProject.text: The type of the project metadata, which must implement

members.name: AddAzureFunctionsProject
members.signature: public static IResourceBuilder<AzureFunctionsProjectResource> AzureFunctionsProjectResourceExtensions.AddAzureFunctionsProject(this IDistributedApplicationBuilder builder, string name, string projectPath)

summary.text: Adds an Azure Functions project to the distributed application.

children.text: method adds an Azure Functions project to the application model using a path to the project file. This allows for projects to be referenced that may not be part of the same solution. If the project path is not an absolute path then it will be computed relative to the app host directory.
When Functions projects are deployed to Azure Container Apps, they are provisioned with the container app
. This enables KEDA auto-scaler rules to be automatically configured based on the Azure Functions triggers defined in the project.

By default, an implicit Azure Storage account is provisioned to be used as host storage for the Functions runtime. This storage account is required by the Azure Functions runtime for operations such as managing triggers, logging function executions, and coordinating instances. The implicit storage account is assigned the following roles:
to specify a custom Azure Storage resource as the host storage instead of the implicit default storage account.

remarks.text: Add an Azure Functions project to the app model via a project path.

returns.text: for the added Azure Functions project resource.

builder.text: to which the Azure Functions project will be added.

name.text: The name to be associated with the Azure Functions project. This name will be used for service discovery when referenced in a dependency.

projectPath.text: The path to the Azure Functions project file.

summary.text: Configures the Azure Functions project resource to use the specified Azure Storage resource as its host storage.

returns.text: The resource builder for the Azure Functions project resource, configured with the specified host storage.

builder.text: The resource builder for the Azure Functions project resource.

storage.text: The resource builder for the Azure Storage resource to be used as host storage.

members.name: WithReference
members.signature: public static IResourceBuilder<AzureFunctionsProjectResource> AzureFunctionsProjectResourceExtensions.WithReference<TSource>(this IResourceBuilder<AzureFunctionsProjectResource> destination, IResourceBuilder<TSource> source, string? connectionName = null)

summary.text: Injects Azure Functions specific connection information into the environment variables of the Azure Functions project resource.

remarks.text: This method is not available in polyglot app hosts. Use the standard
method from the base resource builder instead.

connectionName.text: An override of the source resource's name for the connection name. The resulting connection name will be connectionName if this is not null.

destination.text: The resource where connection information will be injected.

source.text: The resource from which to extract the connection string.

summary.text: Represents an Azure Functions project resource within the Aspire hosting environment.

remarks.text: This class is used to define and manage the configuration of an Azure Functions project, including its associated host storage. We create a strongly-typed resource for the Azure Functions to support Functions-specific customizations, like the mapping of connection strings and configurations for host storage. ///

summary.text: Represents an Azure Functions project resource within the Aspire hosting environment.

remarks.text: This class is used to define and manage the configuration of an Azure Functions project, including its associated host storage. We create a strongly-typed resource for the Azure Functions to support Functions-specific customizations, like the mapping of connection strings and configurations for host storage. ///

summary.text: Represents a Durable Task hub resource. A Task Hub groups durable orchestrations and activities. This resource extends the scheduler connection string with the TaskHub name so that clients can connect to the correct hub.

name.text: The logical name of the Task Hub (used as the TaskHub value).

scheduler.text: The durable task scheduler resource whose connection string is the base for this hub.

summary.text: Represents a Durable Task hub resource. A Task Hub groups durable orchestrations and activities. This resource extends the scheduler connection string with the TaskHub name so that clients can connect to the correct hub.

name.text: The logical name of the Task Hub (used as the TaskHub value).

scheduler.text: The durable task scheduler resource whose connection string is the base for this hub.

summary.text: Gets the connection string expression composed of the scheduler connection string and the TaskHub name.
Gets the parent durable task scheduler resource that provides the base connection string.
Gets the name of the Task Hub. If not provided, the logical name of this resource is returned.
Represents the containerized emulator resource for a
. This is used to host the Durable Task scheduler logic when running locally (e.g. with an Azure Functions emulator).

remarks.text: The emulator resource delegates its annotation collection to the underlying scheduler so that configuration and metadata remain consistent across both representations.

scheduler.text: The underlying durable task scheduler resource that provides naming and annotations.

summary.text: Represents the containerized emulator resource for a
. This is used to host the Durable Task scheduler logic when running locally (e.g. with an Azure Functions emulator).

remarks.text: The emulator resource delegates its annotation collection to the underlying scheduler so that configuration and metadata remain consistent across both representations.

scheduler.text: The underlying durable task scheduler resource that provides naming and annotations.

summary.text: Represents a Durable Task scheduler resource used in Aspire hosting that provides endpoints and a connection string for Durable Task orchestration scheduling.
Represents a Durable Task scheduler resource used in Aspire hosting that provides endpoints and a connection string for Durable Task orchestration scheduling.
Gets the expression that resolves to the connection string for the Durable Task scheduler.
Gets a value indicating whether the Durable Task scheduler is running using the local emulator (container) instead of a cloud-hosted service.
